output.summary: Two changes required: (1) Update test page logo from media ID 6 (favicon.png) to media ID 214 (logo.svg - the full IGNITE logo with bee icon + wordmark). (2) Validate logo sizing against Figma - the current implementation is correct. The logo link uses self-stretch to span both rows and the image is constrained to max-h-[58px] on desktop / max-h-[26px] on mobile, matching the Figma measurement of 57.685px. The issue reported ('logo should take both rows') is a test content problem - the favicon.png (512x512 square) renders as a tiny square at 58px height, while the correct logo.svg (226x58) will fill the space properly as a wide landscape image.

fileChanges.description: Logo styling is already correct - self-stretch on logo link spans both rows, **:max-h-[58px] on desktop and max-md:**:max-h-[26px] on mobile constrain the image to Figma dimensions (57.685px). No code changes needed.
fileChanges.details: The logo link class 'block self-stretch w-auto **:w-auto **:max-w-full **:h-full **:object-contain shrink-0 max-md:**:max-h-[26px] md:**:max-h-[58px]' correctly implements the Figma design where the logo spans the full height of the two-row right column (utility row + nav row with gap-6 between them).

fileChanges.description: PHP logo styling matches TSX - same self-stretch + max-height constraints. No code changes needed.
fileChanges.details: The PHP logo link class is identical to TSX: 'block self-stretch w-auto **:w-auto **:max-w-full **:h-full **:object-contain shrink-0 max-md:**:max-h-[26px] md:**:max-h-[58px]'

designDecisions.question: Does the logo need code changes to span both rows?
designDecisions.recommendation: No. The current implementation already has self-stretch on the logo link (spans the full height of the flex container which includes both rows) and max-h-[58px] on the image (matches Figma's 57.685px). The visual issue is caused by using a square favicon (512x512) as the logo instead of the correct landscape IGNITE logo SVG (226x58). With the correct SVG, the logo will visually span both rows at 58px height and ~226px width.
designDecisions.evidence: Figma node 441:28859 shows logo at 57.685px height x ~225px width (two parts: Layer_1 at 66.844px + Group at 148.601px with 9.541px gap). The logo sits alongside the right column (node 441:28971) which contains utility row and nav row with gap-6 (24px). The logo container has content-stretch (self-stretch) to match the right column height.

designDecisions.question: Should the max-h-[58px] constraint be removed to let the logo grow taller?
designDecisions.recommendation: No. The Figma explicitly shows the logo at 57.685px height. The max-h-[58px] is the correct constraint. The logo appearing small is because the test page uses a square image (favicon.png) which gets constrained to 58x58px. The correct logo SVG (226x58) will fill the width properly at this height. Removing the constraint would break content resilience for non-standard logo uploads.
designDecisions.evidence: Figma node 441:28860 (Layer_1/bee icon): 66.844px wide x 57.685px tall. Node 441:28871 (Group/wordmark): 148.601px wide x 57.685px tall. Both exactly 57.685px tall, confirming the 58px max-height is correct.

testPageChanges.description: Change logoId from 6 to 214 on all header-main blocks in the test page
testPageChanges.details: Media ID 6 is favicon.png (512x512 square bee icon). Media ID 214 is logo.svg (226x58 full IGNITE logo with bee icon + wordmark). All 9 header-main blocks on the test page currently use logoId:6. Update all instances to logoId:214. This is a WordPress content update via the REST API - no code file changes needed.

output.qaNote: After updating the logo, take screenshots at desktop (1440px) and mobile (375px) to verify the logo renders correctly spanning both rows on desktop and constrained on mobile. Compare against Figma screenshot. No functional changes were made - only test page content was updated.
